_about: Documented feedback-resistant (fbr) ground-truth set for E. coli aspartate kinase III (lysC, UniProt P08660). Two provenance buckets: (A) UniProt MUTAGENESIS features of P08660 whose description mentions lysine/feedback/inhibition/desensitization; (B) a small hard-coded classic literature set. EVERY position is VERIFIED against the fetched P08660 sequence (length 449) at test time; mismatches are flagged, not trusted.

ground_truth_uniprot_mutagenesis.note: UniProt MUTAGENESIS K8R: 'less sensitive to lysine inhibition' BUT reduces activity ~98% and raises KM(Asp) ~40x. CATALYTIC N-terminal residue (ATP binding, pos 8-11), not an ACT-domain fbr mutant. Lysine-insensitivity here is a side-effect of crippled catalysis. Source: UniProt P08660 feature.

UniProt MUTAGENESIS D202E: same phenotype as K8R ('less sensitive to lysine inhibition', activity -98%, KM(Asp) +40x). N-terminal/catalytic region (Aspartokinase region 2-245), NOT the ACT regulatory domain. Source: UniProt P08660 feature.

ground_truth_literature.note: Classic lysC fbr allele in the ACT domain. Overlaps annotated L-lysine binding site (residue 318). Ref: Kikuchi Y. et al., FEMS Microbiol Lett 1997; Ogawa-Miyata Y. et al., Biosci Biotechnol Biochem 2001 65:1149.
Classic lysC fbr allele; ACT domain, immediately flanking lysine contacts 321/324. Ref: Kikuchi et al. 1997; Ogawa-Miyata et al. 2001.
Classic lysC fbr allele; coincides with annotated L-lysine binding site (residue 345). One of the most-cited deregulating mutations. Ref: Kikuchi et al. 1997; Ogawa-Miyata et al. 2001.

Classic lysC fbr allele; ACT domain (within 313-394) but NOT an annotated lysine contact (~6-7 residues C-terminal of the 345/346 contact). Ref: Kikuchi et al. 1997; Ogawa-Miyata et al. 2001; widely used in industrial lysine strains.

_literature_positions_rejected.reason: fetched residue at 250 is E, expected T from some numbering conventions -> numbering offset / unverified; EXCLUDED to avoid fabricated ground truth.

fetched residues (E346, V380, V347) do not match the residue identities cited in mixed-numbering literature; EXCLUDED. These positions are likely numbering-convention artifacts (initiator-Met inclusion / alternate reference seq).

_caveats_note: The UniProt MUTAGENESIS bucket for P08660 does NOT contain the classic ACT-domain fbr mutants (M318I/G323D/S345F/T352I); its two lysine-related entries (K8R, D202E) are catalytic-region mutations whose lysine-insensitivity is a side effect of crippled catalysis. The genuine deregulating fbr alleles come entirely from the literature bucket. This bounds what the WHERE-gate is evaluated against and is reported transparently.
